content:
Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council recorded £355.2 million in total spending across 30,580 transactions between 2021/22 and 2025/26.
  • The council's spending data showed no significant anomalies or red flags during automated analysis.
  • No specific supplier names or amounts were flagged for unusual patterns or concerns.
  • The data covered a comprehensive five-year period, providing a robust sample for analysis.

Comprehensive Spending Overview

Burnley Borough Council's spending data for the period 2021/22 to 2025/26 amounts to a substantial £355.2 million. This figure encompasses a wide range of services and operations essential to the functioning of the district council. The data, comprising 30,580 transactions, offers a detailed snapshot of the council's financial activities over five years. Such a comprehensive dataset is crucial for ensuring transparency and accountability in public spending.

The total expenditure of £355.2 million reflects the council's commitment to various public services, including infrastructure, social care, and administrative functions. This amount is distributed across numerous suppliers and service providers, although specific supplier names were not highlighted in the analysis. The absence of notable anomalies suggests a relatively stable and consistent spending pattern over the reviewed period.

Automated Analysis and Findings

Our automated analysis of Burnley Borough Council's spending data did not reveal any significant red flags or irregularities. The analysis included checks for duplicates, split payments, round-number anomalies, and adherence to Benford's Law. These checks are designed to identify potential issues such as fraud, errors, or inefficiencies in the spending process. The fact that no issues were flagged indicates a robust financial management system within the council.

The absence of anomalies does not mean that there is no room for improvement. Continuous monitoring and periodic reviews are essential to maintain the integrity of the council's financial processes. The data's comprehensiveness and the lack of red flags provide a solid foundation for further analysis and potential optimisations in future spending practices.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council about specific spending categories or transactions that interest you.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to stay informed about financial decisions and budget allocations.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to review details of major contracts and suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor to express any concerns or questions about the council's spending and financial management.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis.
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
